items.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
items.summary: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's new clutch king and a Colombian death threat scare
items.summary: Spain's new clutch king saves the day, a record-breaking streak is finally broken and a Colombian midfielder receives death threats. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

items.title: Australia has a deficit dilemma, could means-testing wealth be the solution?

items.title: Aboriginal artist combining centuries-old glassblowing technique with millennia-old traditional weaving practice
items.summary: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

items.title: Former Sydney high school students lead blockbuster movies Moana and Supergirl
items.summary: Some cities have one hometown hero, but Newtown has two making the community proud in a double-feature of blockbuster hits you could watch on the same day.

items.title: Fears workers will never receive entitlement from GFG's liquidated companies
items.summary: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

items.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
items.summary: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

items.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
items.summary: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

items.title: Donald Walker skydives to celebrate his 89th birthday on the Sunshine Coast
items.summary: Donald isn't alone in chasing thrills at an older age, with data from SkyDive Australia showing more than 14,000 people over 50 have dived since 2023.

items.title: After surviving horrific trauma, one woman is helping refugees in Logan heal together
items.summary: Esperance Nyirabarahinyuza survived trauma and losing family. Now, she is helping more than 100 refugee and migrant women in Logan rediscover confidence, community and belonging, one conversation at a time.

items.title: Handmade quilts weave culture into Forster NAIDOC Week baby celebration
items.summary: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

items.title: NT Police release new footage of Bradley John Murdoch ahead of 25th anniversary of Falconio mystery
items.summary: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

items.title: The World Cup just had one of its hottest matches, in a challenge for the sport
items.summary: A 38-degree Celsius evening match has raised fresh questions about how football manages heat in a warming world.

items.title: The Interview: Jessica Mauboy wants to share the culture and community of her home
items.summary: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

items.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
items.summary: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

items.title: NATO was a lesson in how the geopolitical landscape has shifted around Trump
items.summary: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

items.title: BYD backflips and offers full refunds after customers given cars from wrong year
items.summary: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

items.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
items.summary: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

items.title: Telstra faces outage investigation as expert says legal gaps may let it off
items.summary: Telstra faces an outage investigation as an expert says legal gaps may let the telco off the hook.

items.title: Cape Fear TV show, starring Amy Adams and Javier Bardem, has distinctly modern menace
items.summary: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

items.title: All-star cast set sail in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
items.summary: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Jurassic Park, the Scottish play and Wimbledon fruit

items.title: Spain strikes late to seal semi berth after Belgium keeper howler
items.summary: Spain will face France in a World Cup semifinal after beating Belgium 2-1 thanks to a late Mikel Merino goal and a goalkeeping error.

items.title: Relentless Sinner downs Djokovic, Zverev ends Fery's dream run to set up blockbuster Wimbledon final
items.summary: British wildcard Arthur Fery's bid to make Wimbledon history has ended after he was knocked out by Alexander Zverev, who will face off against reigning champion Jannik Sinner in the final.

items.title: Iran denies requesting US talks, while Donald Trump says ceasefire is 'over'

items.title: Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer, tributes paid to 'incredible musician'
items.summary: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up new art gallery in Bangkok, Thailand
items.summary: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

items.title: Bayeux Tapestry returns to Britain for first time in nearly 1,000 years
items.summary: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in Britain for the first time in nearly 1,000 years, travelling under police escort during a meticulously choreographed journey from France.

items.title: Security precaution led Trump to leave Türkiye on his old Air Force One instead of Qatari-gifted jet, sources say
items.summary: Officials reportedly urged the US president to swap out his Qatari-donated luxury jet as a security precaution due to renewed hostilities with Iran.

items.title: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell in collision involving pedestrian

items.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss
items.summary: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

items.summary: Alexander Terry was sentenced to nine years in jail for the rape and sexual assault of four women at a Townsville apartment complex where he was a caretaker.

items.summary: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

items.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
items.summary: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

items.title: Lush native lawn ideas for waterwise, lower-maintenance gardens
items.summary: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

items.title: Australian woman accused of illegally voting in US faces jury trial in Louisiana
items.summary: A Sydney woman will face trial in the US state of Louisiana after being accused of falsely claiming to be a US citizen and voting illegally in two federal elections.

items.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms
items.summary: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Coffee Camp cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees for flood mitigation
items.summary: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

items.title: Charlie Kirk's family requests evidence against accused killer be shown publicly
items.summary: The family of conservative activist Charlie Kirk has pleaded with a judge to allow more public access to evidence presented against the man accused of the assassination.

items.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
items.summary: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

items.title: The Rolling Stones — Foreign Tongues review: a late-career peak in their eighties
items.summary: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

items.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say
items.summary: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

items.title: Derryn Hinch, the 'human headline', former senator and broadcaster, dies aged 82
items.summary: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

items.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
items.summary: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

items.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird for first time
items.summary: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

items.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
items.summary: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

items.title: Man sentenced to six years and five months jail over sex trafficking of Indonesian girl
items.summary: A Sydney judge found 45-year-old Surya Subekti has demonstrated no genuine remorse.

items.title: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady 'deeply sorry' for national outage, says investigations are ongoing
items.summary: Telstra chief executive Vicki Brady apologises to the company's customers, saying the telco let them down and that investigations into the cause of Wednesday's Telstra outage are continuing.

items.title: Defending Evian champion Grace Kim struggles after day one
items.summary: Australians Grace Kim and Minjee Lee both have sluggish starts at the Evian Championship, where Kim rocketed into the major winners' circle with an incredible play-off triumph last year.

items.title: Eight US men charged over alleged terror plot to kill Donald Trump at UFC White House event
items.summary: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

items.title: Traditional owners say connection to country ignored under flawed legislation
items.summary: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

items.title: Australia Post worker charged over alleged mail tampering at Perth Airport
items.summary: Australia Post worker Hussam Kassem is charged by police with mail tampering at a Perth Airport sorting facility, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

items.title: Cruz Hewitt through to Wimbledon juniors semifinal as Aussie duo falls short in mixed doubles final
items.summary: Cruz Hewitt continues to better his famous father's Wimbledon juniors record after yet another convincing win at the All England Club.

items.title: Judge hands down verdict in Jayson Gillham and Melbourne Symphony Orchestra trial
items.summary: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

items.title: Indigenous whale-watching tour shares ancient stories of Illawarra coastline
items.summary: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.
